resources.string: Camera permission is required to use this app.
You are on the latest version.
May your shot be better than your excuses.

string-array.name: insulting_warnings
string-array.item: Physics called. It wants your law degree back.
A fine example of geometric optimism.
Aiming is not a suggestion.
Even the chalk finds your methods dusty.
Let\'s list other things you could be doing instead. There\'s knitting, and…
Talent not detected.
Can\'t even scratch common sense.
There are no shots over there. I checked.
Do you need a bigger phone or a smaller table?
Your Mrs. called, she wants her title back.
\'A do not, this is,\' says Yoda.
Nope.
Not happening.
Won\'t work.
Try harder.
PLEASE try harder.
Try gooder?
Try… better. That\'s it. Not your shot—the word I was looking for.
No.
Physics says no.
God says no.
Your mom says she\'s proud no matter what.
Your father says nothing.
Hey batter, batter…
In the beginning, God created a better shot.
Am I crying from laughing, or is this just sad?
Am I pretty, yet?
Do you even know how to pool, bro?
That\'s… an angle. Not a good one.
I can\'t be more disappointed than your dad.
Consult a physicist. Or a therapist.
Maybe if you put your phone down once in a while....

string-array.name: hater_mode_responses
string-array.item: HaterIcon01
HaterIcon02
HaterIcon03
HaterIcon04

resources.string: How confident do you feel about playing pool or using this app?
Keep it to the basics, please.
Eff you, Dad. I know everything!
The tutorial is over. Go away.

string-array.name: tutorial_dynamic_non_ar
string-array.item: Look at you, breaking free. This is Dynamic Mode. The world is your table.
Your phone is now the cue stick. Rotate it horizontally and vertically to aim. The line follows your gaze.
Tap anywhere on the \'floor\' to move the Target Ball. It\'s like magic, but with math. Tap Finish when you\'re ready.

string-array.name: tutorial_dynamic_ar
string-array.item: AR mode active. Aim your lens at the table. This is the future, or at least a reasonable approximation.
The shot guide line is anchored to the bottom of your screen. Point and shoot, essentially.
Tap on a physical ball on the table. The Target Ball will \'Snap\' right to it. Efficiency is the only virtue here. Tap Finish.

string-array.name: tutorial_beginner_static
string-array.item: This is a pool protractor with a bubble leveler.
In your head, pick the ball you want to hit in, and the pocket where you want it to go.
Hold your phone over the ball you chose to hit in. Fit it inside the YELLOW CIRCLE.
Keep it there, and point the YELLOW LINE at the pocket you chose.
Put your finger under the center dot of the BLUE CIRCLE. This is where you need to aim the cue ball.

string-array.name: tutorial_beginner_dynamic
string-array.item: This mode is the exact same thing as the protractor, except you don\'t need to hold it over the target ball.
Hold your phone upright over the cueball.
Drag the yellow circle over the ball you want to hit in.
Resize the YELLOW CIRCLE to match the target ball, using the zoom slider.
Drag your finger on a clear spot on the screen to aim the YELLOW LINE at your pocket.
The center of the BLUE CIRCLE is where you need to aim the cue ball.

string-array.name: tutorial_general
string-array.item: Alright, let\'s get this over with. This isn\'t a toy. It\'s a precision instrument. Try to keep up. Tap \'Next\'.
That circle with the dot is the Target Ball. Drag it over your object ball. I\'ll wait.

The crosshairs mark the Ghost Ball—that\'s where you *should* hit the cue ball. Drag a single finger *anywhere* to rotate the aim. The line should point to a pocket. You know what a pocket is, I assume.

If you insist, you can toggle the \'Cue Ball\' from the menu. Drag it to where your real cue ball is. The line between it and the Ghost Ball is the shot you\'re supposed to be making. It\'s not rocket science. It\'s just physics.
The slider on the right zooms. The one on the bottom rotates the table. I\'m not going to hold your hand on this one.
In the menu, there\'s a \'Calculate Bank\' option. If you\'re feeling brave, or foolish, press it. We\'ll see how you handle something that requires actual thought.
That\'s the crash course. The rest is a test of your character, or lack thereof. Don\'t embarrass us both. Press Finish.

resources.string: We need a couple of things to get started.
CAMERA: This app works by looking at the table through your lens. Without it, we\'re just a black screen.
LOCATION: This is optional, but it lets us remember which table you\'ve scanned so you don\'t have to re-calibrate every time you come back.
Aim this line at the pocket.
Aim the cue ball at\nthe center of the blue circle.
